---
pagetitle: "Ruwan C. Karunanayaka — Statistical Consulting"
page-layout: full
description: "Statistical consulting by Ruwan C. Karunanayaka, PhD — study design, measurement reliability, AI evaluation audits, and R/Shiny dashboards. Associate Professor of Statistics, University of the Fraser Valley, BC."
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-grid}
::: {}
::: {.eyebrow}
Statistical Consulting
:::

# Statistics you can defend.

::: {.lede}
I'm a PhD statistician and Associate Professor who helps researchers, teams, and organizations design studies properly, model data honestly, and deliver results that hold up to scrutiny — from the first power calculation to the final dashboard.
:::

[Work with me](consulting.qmd){.btn-cta .primary}
[See the tools I build](projects.qmd){.btn-cta .ghost}
:::

::: {}
::: {.card-plain}
### What clients get
A defensible answer, not just a number: the right design for the question, methods chosen for the data rather than habit, uncertainty stated plainly, and a writeup or interactive tool your stakeholders can actually use.
:::
::: {style="margin-top:1.2rem"}
[`study design`]{.tag} &nbsp; [`DOE`]{.tag} &nbsp; [`mixed models`]{.tag} &nbsp; [`reliability`]{.tag} &nbsp; [`AI evaluation`]{.tag} &nbsp; [`R / Shiny`]{.tag}
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.band .dark}
::: {.eyebrow}
Credentials
:::
## Grounded in peer-reviewed work.

::: {.cols-3}
::: {.card-plain}
### Training & position
PhD in Statistics (Simon Fraser University, 2018). Associate Professor, Department of Mathematics & Statistics, University of the Fraser Valley.
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Published methods
Peer-reviewed research in experimental design and applied statistics, with methods independently cited across textile engineering, food science, hydrology, and mining research.
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Accreditation
P.Stat. (Professional Statistician) application under review with the Statistical Society of Canada.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.band}
::: {.eyebrow}
What I help with
:::
## Three services, one standard.

::: {.cols-3}
::: {.card-plain}
### Study & experiment design
Sample size and power, randomization, factorial and fractional-factorial designs, including the baseline parameterization — so the data you collect can actually answer your question.
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Modelling & analysis
Mixed-effects and generalized linear models, measurement reliability, calibration and validation. Documented, reproducible, reviewable.
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Tools & dashboards
Interactive R / Shiny applications that put your model in front of the people who need to act on it — see [live examples](projects.qmd).
:::
:::
:::

::: {.band .dark}
::: {.eyebrow}
Statistics for the AI era
:::
## Your AI is only as good as its evaluation.

Benchmark scores drift with the test harness, top-of-leaderboard gaps sit inside statistical noise, and most evaluation pipelines are never checked for reliability. Before an AI number drives a decision — which model to buy, whether a system is ready to ship — someone should ask whether the measurement itself can be trusted. That's measurement science, and it's what I do.

::: {.cols-3}
::: {.card-plain}
### Evaluation design & audit
Design AI evaluations that answer the question — proper sampling, contamination control, uncertainty on every comparison — or audit the one you have.
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Reliability of judgments
Human raters and LLM-as-judge pipelines are measurement instruments. I quantify their dependability and tell you how many judgments a trustworthy verdict needs.
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Experiments that respect your budget
When every evaluation run costs money, designed experiments get you more answer per dollar than one-at-a-time testing of prompts, configurations, and models.
:::
:::

[AI services in detail →](consulting.qmd){.btn-cta .primary style="margin-top:1.4rem"}
:::

::: {.band}
::: {.eyebrow}
Beyond consulting
:::
## Research, software, and teaching.

::: {.cols-3}
::: {.card-plain}
### Projects
Deployed forecasting apps and open-source software — working demonstrations of the tools I build.
[Explore →](projects.qmd)
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Research
Published work in experimental design and applied statistics, with an active research program in measurement reliability.
[Publications →](research.qmd)
:::
::: {.card-plain}
### Blog
Notes on statistical methodology — how to design, measure, and trust the numbers.
[All posts →](blog.qmd)
:::
:::
:::
